news.title:
Motilal Oswal Alternates closes maiden Private Credit Fund at ₹1,700 Cr, targeting ₹3,000 Cr with ₹1,500 Cr green shoe option.
The fund includes ₹200 Cr commitment from sponsor and affiliates, aimed at supporting mid-market profitable businesses needing flexible capital.

This marks the seventh straight quarter of single-digit PAT growth since June 2020.

news.title:
RBI mandates 100% collateral for broker funding, impacting broking firms like Motilal Oswal, Jio Financial, and Angel One.
New rules effective April 2026 include 50% collateral for exchange guarantees, restricting bank funding for proprietary trading.
news.summary:
RBI mandates 100% collateral for broker funding, impacting broking firms like Motilal Oswal, Jio Financial, and Angel One.
New rules effective April 2026 include 50% collateral for exchange guarantees, restricting bank funding for proprietary trading.

news.title:
India's Margin Trading Facility surged to ₹1.16 lakh crore by Dec 2025, up from ₹24,920 crore in FY23, per PPFAS MF.
Large bank-backed brokers dominate 50% of MTF; Zerodha, Angel One, Motilal Oswal, Bajaj Securities hold 5-6% each.
news.summary:
India's Margin Trading Facility surged to ₹1.16 lakh crore by Dec 2025, up from ₹24,920 crore in FY23, per PPFAS MF.
Large bank-backed brokers dominate 50% of MTF; Zerodha, Angel One, Motilal Oswal, Bajaj Securities hold 5-6% each.

news.title:
MOFSL retains 'Buy' rating for Kalyan Jewellers with a target price of Rs 600 post strong Q3FY26 results.

news.title:
Analysts diverge on AI's impact on Indian IT; Motilal flags risks, Jefferies sees disruption, Macquarie downplays threats.
AI-driven tools like Anthropic's Cowork challenge ERP models, with HCLTech, Infosys, Coforge showing adaptation potential.
news.summary:
Analysts diverge on AI's impact on Indian IT; Motilal flags risks, Jefferies sees disruption, Macquarie downplays threats.
AI-driven tools like Anthropic's Cowork challenge ERP models, with HCLTech, Infosys, Coforge showing adaptation potential.

news.title:
Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd reported 16% YoY growth in Q3FY26 operating profit to ₹611 crores, led by Asset and Private Wealth business growth of 32%.
The company's AUM grew 33% YoY to ₹1.89 lakh crores, with SIP flows reaching ₹4,500 crores. An interim dividend of ₹6/share, up 20%, was declared.
news.summary:
Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd reported 16% YoY growth in Q3FY26 operating profit to ₹611 crores, led by Asset and Private Wealth business growth of 32%.
The company's AUM grew 33% YoY to ₹1.89 lakh crores, with SIP flows reaching ₹4,500 crores. An interim dividend of ₹6/share, up 20%, was declared.
